channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: SSR Iberia comienza una nueva etapa bajo la dirección de Víctor Moreno
item.description: SSR Iberia anuncia oficialmente el nombramiento de Víctor Moreno como nuevo director general en el marco de una nueva etapa para la agencia. Estos cambios se producen tras más de 15 años al frente de SSR Iberia por parte de Prudencio de Luis y Lorenzo y Ángel Zabala Trueba, quienes dejan la dirección al alcanzar la edad de jubilación. Asimismo, Marisa Carrasco se incorpora como nueva directora de Marketing y Comunicación, asumiendo también funciones de coordinación de ventas y relaciones con clientes.

item.title: El dato centra la nueva gestión del riesgo en seguros
item.description: La gestión del riesgo en seguros ha entrado en una nueva fase. Uno de los mensajes más contundentes del informe es que e l riesgo en seguros es hoy, en gran medida, riesgo de datos . Así lo refleja la tercera ‘Encuesta Anual Global sobre Gestión de Riesgos en el Sector Asegurador’ ( ‘The new shape of risk’ ) de EY, realizada en colaboración con el Instituto de Finanzas Internacionales (IIF), que analiza cómo los chief risk officers (CROs) están redefiniendo su papel en un sector cada vez más dependiente de los datos y la tecnología.

item.title: La incertidumbre económica reduce las previsiones de crecimiento del Seguro
item.description: Esta revisión tiene consecuencias para las estimaciones de crecimiento del seguro en España en 2026 y 2027 . El informe ‘Panorama Económico y Sectorial 2026: actualización de previsiones hacia el segundo trimestre’ , publicado el jueves pasado, anticipa un aumento del 6% en las primas de No Vida en 2026 y del 6,6% en 2027 (crecimiento nominal anual). Mapfre Economics ha bajado su previsión de crecimiento para la economía española en 2026 hasta el 2,1%, una décima menos que en sus estimaciones previas.

item.title: Public Adjuster Charged with Stealing $140K in Insurance Payouts
item.description: Micucci was charged with nine counts each of insurance fraud, theft by deception, deceptive business practices, and misappropriation of moneys held on behalf of another. The Bucks County district attorney announced criminal charges against the owner and operator of Advanced Public Adjusters. Micucci, of Bristol, Pennsylvania, is accused of misappropriating approximately $140,000 in insurance claim funds intended for nine victims and their contractors.

item.title: UK Court Orders Samsung to Pay ZTE $392 Million Lump Sum After Patent Trial
item.description: Samsung Electronics must pay a $392 million lump sum for a license to use ZTE’s patents for mobile phones, London’s High Court ruled on Friday in the English leg of the companies’ global licensing dispute. Judge Richard Meade ruled that Samsung pay a lump sum of $392 million after the two companies were unable to agree on the renewal of a previous 2021 deal. The lump sum is more than the maximum $200 million Samsung had argued for, but less than the $731 million ZTE had sought.

item.title: Steel Company Must Spend $12M to Address Contamination at Operation
item.description: A steel producer must spend $12 million to mitigate hazardous waste violations at its Ohio facility. The corrective measures will address several site areas at the Middletown Works, a 2,600-acre integrated steel mill in operation since 1901. The Department of Justice announced a proposed settlement with Cleveland-Cliffs Steel Corporation over releases of hazardous waste at its Middletown Works operation.

item.title: Hippo ‘Off to a Fast Start’ With Reversal of Q1 Loss a Year Ago
item.description: InsurTech Hippo reversed a first-quarter net loss of $47.7 million last year and posted net income of about $7.1 million for the first three months of 2026. “We got off to a fast start in 2026, significantly advancing our strategies on both growth and operational efficiencies,” Rick McCathron, president and CEO, said in a statement. Related: Hippo, Progressive Embark on Strategic Distribution Relationship Gross written premium of $332 million during Q1 increased 58% compared with the same period a year ago.

item.title: Cost of Howden-Driven Talent War Rises to $31M for Brown & Brown
item.description: Brown & Brown acknowledged that the cost of the Howden-driven talent war has risen to $31 million in annual revenue. Brown & Brown, headquartered in Daytona Beach, Fla., previously revealed that 275 of B&B’s former teammates joined Howden’s U.S. Related: Brown & Brown Files Suit Over Alleged Howden Poaching of 200+ Employees During its Q1 2026 analysts’ call on April 28, Watts and Brown discussed the impact of property catastrophe rates on the company’s results.

item.title: Asia nat cat insured losses top US$5bn, economic damage reaches US$65bn in 2025: Swiss Re
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Australia’s ATC acquires Frontier Global to expand financial lines, London market footprint
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Sudarlin Uzir joins Aon Indonesia as head of industry specialties
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Best of Artemis, week ending May 3rd 2026
item.description: Ten most read articles on Artemis.bm, week ending May 3rd 2026: This is not every article published on Artemis during the last week, just the most popular among our readers over the last seven days. Here are the ten most popular news articles, week ending May 3rd 2026, covering catastrophe bonds, ILS, reinsurance capital and related risk transfer topics. To ensure you never miss a thing subscribe to the weekly Artemis email newsletter updates or get our email alerts for every article we publish.

item.title: CISA Weighs Cutting Deadlines to Fix Digital Flaws Amid Worries Over AI
item.description: cybersecurity officials are considering sharply shorter deadlines for fixing critical flaws in government IT systems, amid concerns hackers could exploit them using artificial‑intelligence tools such as Anthropic’s Mythos, people familiar with the matter said. CISA has typically given civilian agencies a three-week deadline to fix such flaws once they are added to the database, according to cybersecurity researcher Glenn Thorpe, although that has recently dropped to around two weeks. The discussions at CISA come as business leaders and the digital security industry grapple with the fallout from the release of more advanced AI models.

item.title: SpaceX Sued for Alleged Home Damage in South Texas
item.description: A group of 80 South Texas plaintiffs are suing Elon Musk’s aerospace company SpaceX, alleging its rocket testing caused “massive” sonic booms that damaged their houses repeatedly over a two-year period. SpaceX has increased its footprint in the South Texas region significantly as it has ramped up the frequency of its rocket launches and size of its operations. Southern District of Texas Court on Thursday, accusing the company of gross negligence and trespassing for loud blasts caused by 11 rocket tests from April 2023 to October 2025.

item.title: Plane Crashes in Texas Hill Country, Killing 5 Pickleball Players
item.description: Five members of a pickleball club who died after the small plane carrying them crashed in Texas are being mourned by a tight-knit community of fellow players. The Texas Department of Public Safety confirmed Saturday that Justin Appling, Hayden Dillard, Brooke Skypala, Stacy Hedrick and Seren Wilson were on board the Cessna 421C that crashed Thursday night in Texas Hill Country. They had traveled to Pro Pickleball Association-sanctioned tournaments all over the country together, from Dallas to Las Vegas.

item.title: InsureMO and DICEUS Partner to Accelerate Insurance Modernization Across Europe, Middle East, and Asia-Pacific
item.description: The post InsureMO and DICEUS Partner to Accelerate Insurance Modernization Across Europe, Middle East, and Asia-Pacific appeared first on Coverager - Insurance news and insights . Singapore & London, April 30, 2026 — InsureMO , the global insurance API, Data, and AI Platform-as-a-Service, and DICEUS, the insurance technology company delivering ready-made platforms for policy administration, underwriting, and customer engagement, today announced a strategic technology partnership spanning Europe, the Middle East, and Asia-Pacific. By combining InsureMO’s API-first platform with DICEUS’s pre-built, modular software suite, the partnership gives insurers a faster path to full-stack modernization without replacing existing systems.

item.title: InsureMO Expands Partner Ecosystem with Upsure’s Core Insurance Suite
item.description: Upsure brings a modular core insurance suite covering policy administration, underwriting, claims, distribution, and policyholder servicing to InsureMO customers. With Upsure now in the InsureMO partner ecosystem, carriers in India, Southeast Asia, the Middle East, and Africa can deploy InsureMO’s connectivity, AI, and product configuration layer alongside Upsure’s configurable core through a single coordinated engagement. SINGAPORE, April 30, 2026: InsureMO , the global insurance platform spanning API, Data, AI, and Partner Apps, today welcomed Upsure (Jiva Business Ventures Pvt.

item.title: belo levanta US$ 14 M y pone en mira segmento B2B
item.description: “La compañía también está trabajando en ampliar sus casos de uso, incluyendo el desarrollo del segmento B2B, con el objetivo de llevar su propuesta a empresas y generar nuevas fuentes de crecimiento”, dijo a iupana Manuel Beaudroit, cofundador y CEO de belo. La fintech argentina belo levantó una serie A de US$ 14 millones, en una ronda liderada por Tether, uno de los principales emisores de stablecoins del mundo. El capital recaudado ayudará para el lanzamiento de nuevas líneas de negocio, como el segmento corporativo.

item.title: Buenas Fuentes: Nubank invertirá casi 9K millones en Brasil
item.description: PicPay de Brasil sufre ajuste tras su IPO PicPay (PICS) registró la peor caída diaria entre las fintechs brasileñas que cotizan en EE. BBVA Colombia habilitará pago de nómina con Bre-B BBVA Colombia habilitará el pago de nóminas empresariales a través de las llaves Bre-B, permitiendo transferencias inmediatas sin importar banco o billetera. La solución mejora liquidez, control y trazabilidad, reduce errores operativos y estará disponible 24/7, con impacto potencial en cerca de 100.000 empresas.

item.title: Berkshire, Cyber Risk and the Strait of Hormuz: Insurability Hinges on Price
item.description: During the recent Berkshire Hathaway annual meeting, Chief Executive Officer Greg Abel told shareholders that the conglomerate’s decisions on investments and acquisitions depend on several factors, highlighting price relative to risk as one of them. “When can you offer insurance to ships crossing the Strait of Hormuz?” CNBC’s Becky Quick asked Jain on behalf of a Berkshire shareholder. “The short answer is it depends on the price,” Jain said, offering a response on the marine risk question that was reminiscent of one offered by Warren Buffett, the former CEO of Berkshire Hathaway, during the 2020 annual meeting.

item.title: Details TBD: Berkshire’s Jain, Abel Describe Tokio Marine Strategic Pact
item.description: Some details of a long-sought-after partnership with Tokio Marine aren’t precisely defined, instead relying on the good faith dealings and reputation of the Japanese insurance giant, Berkshire Hathaway executives said at the conglomerate’s annual meeting. “We’ve tried year after year to get a relationship going with Tokio Marine & [Nichido] Fire,” Berkshire’s vice chair said, referring to the large Japan non-life insurer in Tokio Marine Holdings as a company that is “clearly regarded as a blue-chip company in the international arena.” “Every insurance company would like to be associated with them,” Jain said, noting that getting to a partnership wasn’t easy. “But Tokio Marine, they are a quality company.” In Jain’s view, they do “first-class business in a first-class way,” he said, referencing a phrase he said he borrowed from foundational principles banking giant JP Morgan.

item.title: HDI Global sees ‘substantial’ opportunity for Southeast Asia renewables, charts a new hope for the region’s energy future
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Aon promotes trio to senior APAC commercial roles
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Gallagher Re elevates Jun Liu to head of China agriculture
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: ADB issues US$160m inaugural sovereign cat bonds for Kyrgyz Republic, Tajikistan
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: ADB’s inaugural catastrophe bonds to “pave way for future issuances” – VP Roberta Casali
item.description: Following the successful issuance of the Asian Development Bank’s (ADB) first catastrophe bond offerings, Roberta Casali, Vice-President for Finance and Risk Management at the ADB has explained how the cat bonds will provide committed financing for disasters and said she hopes the first ADB cat bonds will “pave the way for future issuances.” As we reported on April 24th , the Asian Development Bank’s (ADB) first catastrophe bonds had been priced in the market, securing a $160 million capital market-backed source of parametric earthquake and extreme precipitation disaster risk financing, evenly split $80 million each across issuances for beneficiaries the Kyrgyz Republic and Tajikistan . The bonds are designed to protect against severe disasters and provide quick liquidity to the Kyrgyz Republic and Tajikistan following a qualifying event.” Commenting on the rationale for the cat bonds, ADB Vice-President for Finance and Risk Management Roberta Casali explained, “When a major earthquake or flood strikes, it can set back development by years. We’ve been following the ADB’s journey into the catastrophe bond market for the first time since we revealed it was planning its first catastrophe bonds back in November 2025 .

item.title: Gallagher partners with Kita on carbon project risk insights
item.description: The offering uses Kita’s analytics to assess project and portfolio risk, giving buyers, sellers, and investors data-driven insights into the likelihood of project success in a fast-growing but largely unregulated voluntary carbon market. Gallagher has partnered with carbon insurance provider Kita to launch a risk intelligence consulting service for carbon removal projects. “Gallagher’s brokers and advisory teams worldwide can now provide clients with a fast, data-driven view of project and portfolio risk across the carbon removal industry.

item.title: Berkshire Hathaway re/insurance underwriting earnings rose 29% to $1.717bn in Q1 2026
item.description: Berkshire Hathaway, the American multinational conglomerate holding company, has reported increased net underwriting earnings from its insurance and reinsurance businesses of $1.717 billion for the first-quarter of 2026, a roughly 29% increase on the prior year. Helping to bolster performance of the insurance and reinsurance underwriting earnings generated by Berkshire Hathaway was an absence of major catastrophe loss impacts in the quarter, with no significant catastrophe events (exceeding $150 million per event) experienced in the period. But, Berkshire Hathaway has reported lower underwriting earnings in its reinsurance business and auto-specialist insurer GEICO for Q1 2026, Earnings were stronger this year in the property and casualty (P&C) reinsurance segment for Berkshire Hathaway in the first-quarter of this year, where its National Indemnity Company (NICO), General Re and TransRe groups are the main underwriting companies.

item.title: Funeral Directors Life names Gavin Patrick as Market Center Manager
item.description: Funeral Directors Life (FDL) appointed Gavin Patrick as Market Center Manager in Indiana. I’m excited to join the FDL Sales Team and represent the white-glove service FDL is known for.” – Gavin Patrick, Market Center Manager. Gavin has a natural way of building meaningful relationships and taking ownership, and I’m confident he’ll be a strong advocate for funeral homes throughout Indiana.” – Kris Seale, President and CEO of Funeral Directors Life.

item.title: Leave Becomes Engagement Infrastructure at Guardian
item.description: (Image source: Guardian Absence Solutions homepage.) Guardian Life Insurance Company of America (New York) is reframing absence management around a simple but consequential idea: leave is no longer an administrative process. The result, Vanscavish suggests, is that absence is evolving into a form of engagement infrastructure—connecting income protection, regulatory execution, and employee experience in a single continuum. Guardian’s expansion of its paid leave rider—now available in 40 states—is one response to this environment.

item.title: You can’t insure your way out of a heatwave
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: The Week’s 10 Biggest Funding Rounds: Defense Tech Leads With Multiple Large Deals, Topped By $600M For Space Security Startup True Anomaly
item.description: venture deals this week were led by a massive defense tech raise for space security startup True Anomaly . True Anomaly develops space security and in-orbit defense systems, an area drawing increasing venture investor attention amid rising geopolitical tensions. True Anomaly , $600M, aerospace and defense: Centennial, Colorado-based True Anomaly raised a massive $600 million Series D led by Eclipse and Riot Ventures , with participation from a long list of other backers including Accel , 645 Ventures , G Squared , Acme Capital , Menlo Ventures and Paradigm .

item.title: Aclaimant and NFP Expand RMIS Partnership
item.description: Aclaimant (Chicago) has been added to the preferred risk management information system (RMIS) partner list of NFP (New York), formalizing an existing relationship between the two companies. “NFP and Aclaimant have spent years in the field together… Being named a preferred RMIS partner reflects what those clients have already experienced.” NFP clients will have access to Aclaimant’s safety, incident and claims management workflows alongside advisory and placement services. Combining Claims Expertise, Modern Workflows and Better Data “We’re excited to continue our partnership with Aclaimant and build on the success we’ve had helping clients run risk management more effectively,” says Jeff Stagg, Chief Claims Officer, NFP.

item.title: VisitorsCoverage Launches ChatGPT Travel Insurance App
item.description: VisitorsCoverage (Santa Clara, Calif.) has launched an application within ChatGPT designed to support travel insurance research and purchasing through a conversational interface. “Integrating our services into the ChatGPT ecosystem is a natural step in our journey to meet travelers where they are, providing them with the tools they need to secure the best protection through the power of AI.” VisitorsCoverage says the launch reflects its focus on expanding digital access to insurance services for travelers. The application enables users to describe travel needs, receive insurance quotes and compare plans across multiple carriers.

item.title: SAS CTO Frames AI Moment Around Human Relevance, Trust
item.description: Will people matter?” The framing set the tone for a speech that positioned the current wave of artificial intelligence not as a technological crisis, but as a test of how organizations define the role of human judgment in an increasingly automated world. A crisis of confidence, of human ingenuity,” Harris said. “The way you show up right now with AI will determine your brand value to the next generation,” he said, adding that “it’s not about choosing between people and profit.” Instead, he framed AI as the latest in a series of technologies that extend human capability.

item.title: LA Fire Suspect Angry About No Date for New Year’s, Prosecutors Say
item.description: Prosecutors said the man they charged with starting the deadly 2025 Palisades Fire in Los Angeles was upset about a failed relationship and not having New Year’s Eve plans just before he set a small blaze which rekindled six days later. Jonathan Rinderknecht, 30, who is set to go to trial June 8, “exhibited extreme anger, indignation, and frustration about being unable to find companionship on New Year’s Eve,” prosecutors in the office of Los Angeles U.S. Prosecutors submitted a 25-page memo to the court Wednesday outlining the evidence they seek to use at trial, including statements Rinderknecht made to investigators and expert testimony about the fire’s cause.

item.title: Iran Delivers New Proposal to US as Hormuz Remains Shut
item.description: IRNA didn’t elaborate on what the Iranian proposal entailed on key issues such as Hormuz or Iran’s nuclear activities. Iran delivered a new proposal to the U.S. Tehran relayed its latest position to Washington via Pakistan, which mediated a first round of direct negotiations last month, Iran’s state-run Islamic Republic News Agency said on Friday.

item.title: Ford Recalling 180,000 Vehicles for Loose Bolt in Front Seat Frame
item.description: due to a loose bolt in the front seat frame, the U.S. Ford is recalling 179,698 vehicles in the U.S. A seat with a dislodged bolt may not properly restrain an occupant in a crash, NHTSA said.

item.title: Texas Tornado Collapsed Buildings, Ripped Roofs Off Homes
item.description: And all of my stuff go into the sky.” Officials confirmed that a tornado on Tuesday tore through this small Texas city, sending five people to a hospital as it flattened buildings used for manufacturing and ravaged nearby homes. Allison Prater, a National Weather Service meteorologist in Fort Worth, said the tornado touched down in Mineral Wells with winds of at least 120 mph (193 kph). Stormy week kills at least 3 people in Texas, Michigan Violent weather has been plaguing parts of the South and Midwest.

item.title: Portugal Launches $26.5B Resilience Plan After Storms, Blackout
item.description: The plan was initiated after severe storms hit central mainland Portugal in January and February, causing damage worth an estimated 5.3 billion euros, and a crippling power outage in Spain and Portugal exactly a year ago. Presenting the plan, Prime Minister Luis Montenegro said Portugal had been “hit hard in recent years by extreme weather events — droughts, heavy rain, floods and fires — occurring year after year with increasing destructive force”. The initiative, named Portugal Transformation, Recovery and Resilience, seeks to strengthen infrastructure, institutions, homes and businesses against threats linked to climate change, energy security, seismic activity and cyberattacks.

item.title: Three arrested in FCA investigation into suspected unlawful financial promotions
item.description: Three people have been arrested as part of a crackdown on suspected illegal financial promotions. The investigation is ongoing, and further updates will be provided in due course. Two homes in the Chelmsford and Romford areas were searched, as part of an operation led by the FCA and the Eastern Regional Special Operations Unit (ERSOU), a specialist policing unit that tackles serious and organised crime.

item.title: Capital Partners an “important source of resilience” for RenaissanceRe: CEO O’Donnell
item.description: RenaissanceRe’s third-party capital and insurance-linked securities management unit Capital Partners is considered an “important source of resilience” for the company and a key differentiator, especially for clients that “value, scale, reliability and flexibility,” according to CEO Kevin O’Donnell. “That is another important source of resilience, and remains a clear differentiator for RenaissanceRe, especially in markets where clients value, scale, reliability and flexibility.” RenRe’s CFO Bob Qutub also discussed the important contribution of third-party and ILS capital fee income for the reinsurer, saying he anticipates the run-rate continuing. Speaking during RenaissanceRe’s first-quarter earnings call yesterday, O’Donnell explained that the RenaissanceRe Capital Partners division continues to deliver as expected, driving attractive earnings through fee income that complements the reinsurer’s investment and underwriting earnings.

item.title: Technology adoption and AI expands addressable markets, access to capital: Aon CEO Case
item.description: Adoption of advanced technology and artificial intelligence are serving to expand addressable markets for the insurance and reinsurance industry, with digital infrastructure one example where data analytics are expected to help Aon access institutional sources of capital for its clients, CEO Greg Case explained today. Importantly, he explained that the adoption of AI and advanced technology helps Aon unlock more and more diverse sources of capital and capacity for clients, including from the institutional capital markets. “Here, again, our investment and progress in AI embedded analytics is allowing us to expand beyond the $4.6 trillion of traditional insurance capital to access the $250 trillion capital pool that includes private equity, sovereign wealth and pension funds.” Later in the call Case went on to highlight how technology is helping Aon expand its opportunity set.

item.title: Non-life run-off deal momentum remained strong in Q1’26, says PwC
item.description: PwC has reported that legacy market momentum continued in the opening three months of 2026, with nine publicly disclosed non-life run-off deals announced by five acquirers, with cumulative gross liabilities of approximately $730 million transacted for the five transactions that disclosed deal values. “We expect to see more transactions of this size being announced during the course of 2026,” says PwC in its Non-life insurance run-off deals – Q1 2026 review. “This marks a significant step in the evolution of the Australian run-off market, with the presence of another major global acquirer expected to support future deal flow,” reads the report.

item.title: Willis introduces Merger Protect to address US antitrust review costs in M&A
item.description: Willis, the advisory, broking and solutions firm, has introduced Merger Protect, a specialised insurance offering designed to assist organisations in managing the financial implications of US antitrust regulatory reviews during mergers and acquisitions. Willis explains that Merger Protect is intended to reimburse specified costs arising when a Second Request is issued under the Hart-Scott-Rodino Act by the US Federal Trade Commission or the Department of Justice, and, where relevant, during any subsequent enforcement action. “Merger Protect gives deal parties something they haven’t had before: a way to protect against the cost volatility of regulatory review without compromising their ability to defend the transaction.

item.title: AI advancing faster than expected as AIG builds multi-agentic solution: CEO Zaffino
item.description: Peter Zaffino, Chairman and CEO of AIG, said artificial intelligence (AI) has advanced at a faster pace than expected over the past nine months, as he outlined the company’s next phase of AI deployment, focused on developing a multi-agentic solution with an orchestration layer coordinating specialised AI agents. “As a logical next step in our AI deployment, we’re creating a multi-agentic solution with a strong orchestration layer that coordinates specialised and trained AI agents to seamlessly supplement our underwriters analysis and should further augment our underwriters ability to assess risks and rate, and provide coverage with real time alignment. “While we expected the technology would develop meaningfully over time, we could not have predicted the rapid pace of advancement over the last nine months, or the breadth of AI’s potential application.” Zaffino underscored the success of AIG Assist, its generative AI-powered tool, particularly in Lexington middle market property, where it has helped deliver a 30% increase in quoted submissions, reduced time to quote for underwriters by 55%, and increased binding of submissions by approximately 40%.

item.title: Charlie Crist is Running for St. Petersburg Mayor
item.description: Charlie Crist is seeking yet another office, this time running for mayor of his hometown of St. The current mayor, Ken Welch, is the first Black person in the job and is running for reelection. Petersburg mayor’s office is a nonpartisan election, and around a half dozen other candidates are in the race.

item.title: Protective Life to Acquire P/C Specialty Insurance Carrier Obsidian
item.description: “By bringing Obsidian into Protective, we are adding a high-quality specialty insurance platform that expands where and how we grow while staying true to our vision to protect more people through life’s many moments.” The transaction is expected to close in the fourth quarter of 2026 or first quarter of 2027. In a rare crossing of insurance lines, Alabama-based Protective Life Insurance Co. is moving to expand into property/casualty lines by acquiring Obsidian Insurance Holdings, Inc., a New York-based property, casualty and specialty insurance platform.

item.title: Inszone Acquires Arkansas’ Mena Insurance Agency
item.description: Inszone Insurance Services announced the acquisition of Mena Insurance Agency, Inc. This strategic acquisition brings a century-old institution and an experienced team into the Inszone organization, further expanding its footprint in Arkansas. McLafferty, Mena Insurance has been a cornerstone of the local community for over a century.

item.title: Ategrity grows 23% in Q1, targets overlooked E&S niches
item.description: The company continues to avoid large, catastrophe-exposed property risks, instead targeting small and mid-sized businesses in overlooked niches. Ategrity Specialty reported first quarter 2026 results with strong growth and improved underwriting performance, alongside a continued push into underserved segments of the excess and surplus market. During the quarter, Ategrity launched new regional strategies in Texas, Florida, and New England, including a focus on wholesale trade along the I-10 corridor in Texas and older mixed-use properties in Springfield, Massachusetts.
